summary: Deprecated audit trail for WAP Push Message Routing Service. Microsoft's Windows IoT Enterprise service guidance lists the service with `No guidance`, so the current disable action is kept as reference only.

setting.name: WAP Push Message Routing Service
setting.casual_explanation: This service routes Wireless Application Protocol push messages and helps synchronize some device-management sessions.
setting.why_people_change_it: People disable it on systems where mobile-management or push-message features are not used and they want a leaner service set.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Turning it off may reduce an unused service, but Microsoft's service guidance does not classify it as safe to disable, so the practical side effects need more review.
setting.professional_notes: Microsoft's Windows IoT Enterprise service guidance lists WAP Push Message Routing Service `(dmwappushservice)` with a default start mode of `Automatic` and marks it `No guidance`. A local SCM snapshot confirms the exact dmwappushservice identity on the review host: display name `Device Management Wireless Application Protocol (WAP) Push message Routing Service`, binary path `C:\WINDOWS\system32\svchost.exe -k netsvcs -p`, and current start type `3 DEMAND_START`. The app currently disables the service through a service start-mode tweak, but this dataset does not publish that as a validated preset without stronger Microsoft guidance.

allowed_values.label: Windows default
allowed_values.meaning: The WAP Push Message Routing service keeps Microsoft's documented default mode.

allowed_values.label: Service disabled
allowed_values.meaning: The service no longer starts, but Microsoft's service guidance does not classify this as safe to disable.

app_current_implementation.notes: The app disables dmwappushservice by setting its start mode to Disabled, but Microsoft's service guidance provides no disable recommendation.

windows_defaults.label: Microsoft documented default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows systems using the normal WAP Push Message Routing service configuration

states.rationale: Microsoft lists dmwappushservice with an Automatic default start mode.

windows_defaults.notes: This review record keeps the documented default baseline until stronger guidance exists.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: Systems that may use management-session or push-message plumbing
avoid_for: None by default until stronger evidence exists

states.rationale: Keep the documented default because Microsoft's service guidance gives no disable recommendation.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want to stay aligned with the documented Windows default.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You are trying to publish a validated disable preset for this service today.
tradeoffs: The documented default behavior is preserved.
The current app disable action remains documented but not validated.

recommended_profiles.label: Current app profile

states.rationale: Document the current app behavior without validating it as a recommended state.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You are documenting the current product behavior for follow-up review.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You need a Microsoft-backed safe-disable classification.
tradeoffs: The control surface itself is real and reversible.
Microsoft has not classified the service as OK to disable in the service guidance.

evidence.title: Microsoft Learn: Guidance on configuring system services - WAP Push Message Routing Service
evidence.summary: Microsoft lists WAP Push Message Routing Service `(dmwappushservice)` with start mode `Automatic` and marks it `No guidance`.

evidence.title: Local SCM snapshot - dmwappushservice
evidence.summary: Checked-in `sc qc dmwappushservice` snapshot from Microsoft Windows 11 Pro 10.0.26200 confirming the exact service identifier, display name, binary path, and current START_TYPE line on the review host. This proves the exact SCM control surface the app targets.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider disables the `dmwappushservice` service through the batch service start-mode tweak.

decision.why: The service control surface is official, but Microsoft's service guidance does not classify dmwappushservice as safe to disable, so the tweak stays in review.
blocking_issues: Official Microsoft service guidance marks dmwappushservice as No guidance rather than OK to disable.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: SERVICE_NAME: dmwappushservice | START_TYPE : 3 DEMAND_START | DISPLAY_NAME: Device Management Wireless Application Protocol (WAP) Push message Routing Service | BINARY_PATH_NAME: C:\WINDOWS\system32\svchost.exe -k netsvcs -p
validation_proof.notes: The local SCM snapshot proves the exact service identity and control surface on the review host. Microsoft's official guidance evidence remains the basis for the `No guidance` blocker.
